perturbation.original_text: A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic with the dimensions not to exceed 125 x 125 pixels and 20Kb in size. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.
perturbation.changed_text: A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific dimensions and size limitations for the 'Portal', the contract becomes vague about the actual specifications of the portal. This creates an internal contradiction because while the Portal is defined, there is no constraint on the dimension, size of the graphic to be displayed, which can cause uncertainty and conflict during contract enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, Tickets shall be MP3.com's exclusive partner/source for sports, entertainment, and travel tickets, and MP3.com shall include a Tickets Portal on the "Music" page and the "Pop," "Rock" and "Alternative" genre pages on the Website. A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Portal' with specific dimensions and size limitations, while the changed text removes these specifications. The contradicted text refers to the 'Portal' but, due to the omission in the changed text, lacks the specific dimensions and size which might be relevant for enforcing the contract. This omission creates uncertainty and a potential conflict regarding the characteristics of the Portal, leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: MP3.com agrees to deliver a guaranteed minimum of 3,000,000 Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement. An "Impression" is defined as the display of the Tickets Portal to a user on one of the above referenced pages.
perturbation.changed_text: MP3.com agrees to deliver Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement. An "Impression" is defined as the display of the Tickets Portal to a user on one of the above referenced pages.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the minimum value of impressions that MP3.com is required to deliver, it becomes unclear what the expected performance of MP3.com should be. This is an in-text contradiction because it removes the explicit expectation and opens the door for disputes about whether the contract is being fulfilled, making the enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MP3.com agrees to deliver Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement. An "Impression" is defined as the display of the Tickets Portal to a user on one of the above referenced pages.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a guaranteed minimum of 3,000,000 impressions. The changed text removes this guaranteed minimum, leading to an ambiguity in the contract as to what constitutes fulfillment of the impression delivery obligation. The contradicted text states that 'MP3.com agrees to deliver Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement.' Without the specified minimum, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text because it is unclear what level of 'Impressions' is required, potentially leading to disputes and making the agreement unenforceable, thus falling under 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any late payments under this Agreement will be assessed a service fee of one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, to the extent allowed by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Any late payments under this Agreement will be assessed a service fee, to the extent allowed by law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific percentage (1.5%) for late payment fees, the contract now lacks a defined amount for the service fee. This introduces an in-text contradiction, because although a late payment fee is still mentioned, its quantification is omitted, leading to ambiguity and disputes about the actual fee amount, thus creating uncertainty in contract enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Tickets agrees to pay MP3.com, during the term of this Agreement, as follows: (i) $[***] payable on the Effective Date; (ii) $[***] payable on or before one month subsequent to the Effective Date; (iii) $[***] payable on or before two months subsequent to the Effective Date; (iv) $[***] payable on or before three months subsequent to the Effective Date; (v) $[***] payable on or before four months subsequent to the Effective Date; (vi) $[***] payable on or before five months subsequent to the Effective Date. Any late payments under this Agreement will be assessed a service fee, to the extent allowed by law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 1.5% late payment fee. The changed text removes this specific percentage, leaving the fee amount undefined. The contradicted text mentions late payment fees within the same agreement, creating an in-text contradiction because the amount is now unspecified in one place but implied as quantifiable in another, per Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
